query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "infinite worlds with versatile interactions" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on agent-based simulation for retail management and word-of-mouth dynamics, which is an off-domain application of simulation techniques unrelated to the user's specific question about extending the "Infinite Worlds with Versatile Interactions" (llmXive) computer science framework. It does not address the mechanism, variables, or empirical setting of the llmXive system, nor does it serve as a foundational reference for that specific research line.
